what_it_does: It is a complete terrain map of the Channels cross-session fabric and the Voice trial stack, cataloging source files, data schemas, network ports, Python venvs, systemd units, pre-downloaded models, secrets, live status, and notable design findings.
observations: Channels are built around a Node.js MCP server (company_channel.mjs) that uses a dynamic, OS-assigned port and registers each session under .data/channels/<handle>.json.
The channel fabric stores a durable 6.4 MB append-only mailbox at .data/channels/_mail.jsonl plus a thread index at _threads.json.
Live channel registrations exist for ch-*.json, operator.json, and tim.json as of 2026-06-28.

Voice exposes six TTS engines on ports 4123-4128 (Kokoro, Chatterbox, Orpheus, CosyVoice, XTTS, Qwen3-TTS) and seven STT providers (whisper.cpp on :2022, parakeet :2031, canary :2032, granite :2033, moonshine :2034, parakeet-onnx :2035, plus cloud assemblyai).
Moonshine replaced whisper.cpp as the boot-default STT ear on 2026-06-28.
The reference clip COMPANY_VOICE_REF does not exist yet; clone engines (Chatterbox, XTTS, CosyVoice) fail loud if it is absent.
CosyVoice import is currently blocked by an openai-whisper wheel / deepspeed build failure.
VRAM control was converged on 2026-06-06 so voice/lifecycle.py drives the same systemd units and budgets through ops/cli/gpu.py, closing a prior dual-authority OOM bug.
A universal speakable.py layer normalizes markdown and canonical expression tags (<laugh>, <sigh>, etc.) into engine-native syntax before TTS.
The code derives TTS engine ports and loadable voice services from ops/services.json at import time and fails loud if entries are missing.
Claude ancestor PID is traced by walking /proc so the supervisor can resolve its own session.
Only one secret is referenced: ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY in the gitignored .secrets file; no TTS keys are used.
standouts: Unified VRAM authority convergence: lifecycle.py and the console now use the same systemd units and shared gpu.py budget, preventing OOM dual-authority races.
Moonshine is the new boot-default ear: ONNX-runtime, <1 GB, 0 VRAM, ~9x realtime, chosen on 2026-06-28.
The reference clip is missing, which will block three of the six TTS engines until voice/ops/make_reference_clip.sh mints it from Qwen3-VoiceDesign.
No silent fallback on a down STT ear: transcribe() raises loud naming the provider and endpoint, forcing operator awareness.
A single speakable() function is the pre-TTS seam for every reply-to-speech path, centralizing engine-aware expression mapping.
conventions: Configuration is env-driven: ports, handles, supervisor address, root dir, and device choices all come from environment variables.
Registry-is-truth: ops/services.json is the single source for engine ports and loadable services; code fails loud if the projection is incomplete.
Fail-loud over silent fallback: missing reference clip, down ear, unreadable VRAM, or missing registry entries all raise rather than degrade silently.
Systemd user units are the launch primitive for heavy voice services; shared HTTP shells (_service.py, _stt_service.py) keep wrappers small.
Lazy imports and per-engine warm() functions isolate heavy model loading.
Durable append-only logging for channel replies and thread indexing.
Secrets are read from a gitignored .secrets file with env override; no keys are hardcoded.
Canonical expression tags emitted by the brain are mapped per engine rather than each engine parsing its own syntax.
concerns: COMPANY_VOICE_REF is absent, so Chatterbox, XTTS, and CosyVoice cannot actually serve cloned voice until post-restart minting.
CosyVoice is documented as failing to import due to an openai-whisper wheel / deepspeed timeout, making it unusable.
A 16 GB GPU cannot host the brain, embeddings, and multiple GPU STT ears simultaneously; the resident-vs-on-demand policy is noted but not automated.
The 6.4 MB _mail.jsonl has no rotation or query mechanism described in this file.
The channel supervisor at http://127.0.0.1:8771 is assumed running but its own source is not shown here.
Most systemd voice units are on-demand and not auto-started post-boot; live verification is pending.
Multiple ch-*.json registrations suggest parallel sessions, but concurrency/identity rules are not detailed.
notes: A live, structured inventory of the Channels MCP fabric and the six-engine/six-ear Voice trial, meant to be read before debugging, restarting, or fusing these subsystems.
questions: Where is the source for the channel supervisor that listens on :8771 and pushes replies back to sessions?
How is the _chat_lead.txt marker used, and what does its 11-byte content mean?
What process decides which GPU ear is resident versus on-demand, and how is that enforced?
How is the 6.4 MB _mail.jsonl queried, rotated, or truncated over time?
Is whisper.cpp installed on the host, or is it expected to be provided externally?
What is the exact schema/content of the profile object inside ch-*.json registrations?
What triggers and records the post-restart verification checklist from voice/ops/README.md?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file declares itself a complete terrain map for channels + voice and delivers exactly that — file lists, port tables, venv status, systemd units, data layout, and design notes.
apparent_themes: Unified resource authority to avoid dual-control bugs
Voice as a modular trial bench of interchangeable engines and ears
Local-first / self-hosted stack with optional cloud STT
Durable, append-only logging for audit and recovery
intention_signals: It is trying to create a single, operator-readable ground truth for a sprawling multi-process subsystem so that restarts, debugging, and fusion integration can proceed without re-reading every source file.
novelty: The combination of a dynamic-port MCP cross-session channel fabric with a parallel trial bench of six TTS engines and six STT ears, all tied together by a universal speakable normalization layer and a unified VRAM authority.
contribution: It contributes a structured, factual inventory of the Channels and Voice subsystems — including interfaces, ports, venv health, live status, current blockers, and hard-won design fixes — that a fused system can use to understand, maintain, and integrate these components without re-deriving the terrain.
summary_for_embedding: Area D terrain map documenting the Channels cross-session MCP fabric (dynamic-port server, session registry, durable mailbox) and the Voice trial stack (six TTS engines on ports 4123-4128, seven STT ears on ports 2022/2031-2035, seven venvs, ten systemd units, env/config-driven, with Moonshine as default ear and a missing reference clip blocking clone engines).
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a complete, operator-readable ground-truth map of the Channels and Voice subsystems for debugging, onboarding, and fusion integration.
